definitions: To prepare (oneself or something) for something difficult or unpleasant.
To make (a structure) stronger or more stable by supporting it.
To press one's body or part of one's body firmly against something in order to stay steady.
To give support or stability to (something) with a brace or something similar.
To invigorate or refresh (oneself or someone else), especially with something cold.
examples: She had to brace herself for the bad news.
The builders will brace the old wall.
He braced his feet against the dashboard.
The carpenter used a beam to brace the roof.
The cold shower helped to brace him.
